$comment: LLM corpus generation matrix. Each sample cell = (agent, model) × language × task × size × wrapping, with a seeded topic. The roster pins explicit (agent, model) pairs — one subagent-skill invocation per pair, never --agent all — and the pair is recorded per sample in the manifest so corpus composition stays reproducible.

wrappingStyles.label: raw
wrappingStyles.instruction: Return only the code itself with no surrounding explanation and no markdown fences.

wrappingStyles.label: markdown
wrappingStyles.instruction: Explain your design briefly before the code and add usage examples after it, using markdown fenced code blocks, as you would in a chat answer.

wrappingStyles.label: chat
wrappingStyles.instruction: Answer conversationally with multiple fenced code snippets interleaved with explanations and bullet lists, as in a chat reply.

codeTasks.template: Implement {topic} in {language} with a clean public API, {size}. {wrapping}
Write unit tests in {language} for a module that implements {topic}, {size}. {wrapping}
Write a {language} module implementing {topic} that contains one subtle bug, then show the corrected version of the whole file, {size}. {wrapping}
Write a deliberately clumsy {language} implementation of {topic} and then refactor it into idiomatic {language}, {size}. {wrapping}
Explain {topic} to an intermediate developer with {language} code examples, {size}. {wrapping}
Write idiomatic {language} boilerplate for {topic} (configuration, CLI scaffold, or API client as appropriate), {size}. {wrapping}
Write a short {language} snippet implementing {topic}, then code-review it and reply with concrete improvement suggestions, {size}. {wrapping}
Write {topic} in Python, then translate it to idiomatic {language}, showing both versions, {size}. {wrapping}

nlTasks.template: Answer this technical question in {locale}: how does {topic} work in practice? {size}.
Write an article about {topic} in {locale} and then summarize it in three paragraphs, {size}.
You are a technical writer. Write user-facing documentation in {locale} for a {topic} feature, including an overview, a parameter table, request/response JSON examples, and caveats, {size}.
Draft a professional business email in {locale} about {topic}, followed by a polite reply, {size}.
Explain {topic} in {locale} to a complete beginner with everyday analogies, {size}.
Write a plausible multi-turn (6+ turns) chat exchange in {locale} between a user and an assistant about {topic}, {size}.

codeTopics: a CSV import pipeline with date parsing
a retry wrapper with exponential backoff
a config loader with environment overrides
a pagination helper for a REST API
